channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Microsoft joins AI cost-cutting trend by relying more on its own models
item.description: Microsoft is the latest Silicon Valley giant to cut back on its AI spending.

item.title: Sony propose jusqu’à 50 % de réduction sur le PlayStation Plus pour retenir les joueurs qui boycottent
item.description: Des joueurs qui résilient leur PlayStation Plus pour protester contre la fin des jeux physiques se voient proposer jusqu’à 50 % de réduction pour rester. Une parade de rétention aussi vieille que les abonnements.

item.title: Hasbro and Scooby Doo team up for one of the goofiest Transformers yet
item.description: Hasbro and Scooby Doo team up for one of the goofiest Transformers yet.

item.title: Discord admits AI moderation bug wrongfully banned users over harmless images
item.description: The company confirmed that the issue had been affecting accounts since May, with an additional 200 users banned over the weekend before its team identified and fixed the problem.

item.title: Un simple câble HDMI permet de pirater un ordinateur coupé d’internet à une vitesse record
item.description: Des chercheurs ont trouvé comment voler des données sur un ordinateur totalement coupé d'internet, en se servant du simple câble entre la carte graphique et l'écran. Et à une vitesse jamais atteinte jusqu'ici.

item.title: Can AI fill prescriptions? Here’s what doctor’s think of Utah’s refill program
item.description: Alarm bells are going off for doctors, lawyers and public health experts. A prescription refill program that quietly launched in Utah earlier this year has kicked off a big medical debate: Is artificial intelligence ready to take over tasks that, until now, could only be performed by doctors?

item.title: Une étude bouscule tout : Uranus et Neptune pourraient cacher un océan de magma !
item.description: La mission Voyager 2 de la Nasa a fourni des renseignements sur le champ magnétique, le rayonnement et le champ de gravitation des planètes Neptune et Uranus. Ces informations dépendent de la structure interne de ces planètes et, jusqu'à présent, elles soutenaient les modèles faisant de ces...

item.title: OpenAI teams with Work Louder to launch Codex-native keyboard, weeks after CEO of Apps told staff 'not to be distracted by side quests’
item.description: OpenAI's latest move seems to contradict its earlier stance of not getting distracted by 'side quests'.

item.title: Doom developer id Software is reportedly losing half its staff
item.description: Amid shakeups at Xbox, Doom developer id Software is reportedly losing half its staff.

item.title: How to watch Switzerland vs. Colombia online for free
item.description: How to watch World Cup for free. Live stream Switzerland vs. Colombia in the 2026 FIFA World Cup for free from anywhere in the world.

item.title: Jude Bellingham and Erling Haaland are taking their bromance to the World Cup stage
item.description: The internet is revisiting Bellingham and Haaland’s friendship before the England vs. Norway World Cup match.

item.title: The FCC wants to make easier for ISPs to hide junk fees
item.description: The FCC is set to defang rules around ISP junk fee reporting.

item.title: Sick of trying to find a USB and Windows key? Microsoft has just made reinstalling Windows 11 less painful
item.description: Windows 11 reinstallations will be available via the cloud, which includes appropriate drivers and doesn't require a USB drive.

item.title: After a brief slump, iRobot announced 5 new Roombas and a wet dry vacuum. Is it redemption arc time?
item.description: No, iRobot didn't go out of business after bankruptcy. On July 7, iRobot announced five new Roomba robot vacuums and a cordless mop.

item.title: Google’s Pixel event is set for August 12
item.description: Google's upcoming event in August will introduce new Pixel devices.

item.title: Figma acquires team behind a vibe-coding app
item.description: The Y Combinator-backed company started a vibe-coding platform and later built an agent-creation product.

item.title: 4K Blu-ray fans, it's back — Arrow Video has knocked up to 50% off its discs at multiple retailers including Amazon and I've picked the top 6 discs I'd recommend
item.description: Arrow Films is having a sale on its 4K Blu-rays, with up to 50% off some discs. Here are the 6 I'd pick, all of which we've tested here at TechRadar.

item.title: Here's what the CE mark means on your electronics
item.description: Know when and why the CE mark appear on a label.

item.title: Why is everyone talking about John Oliver and ‘General Hospital’?
item.description: An unexpected guest appearance has become one of the soap’s biggest moments this week. Most people know John Oliver as the Emmy-winning host of HBO’s Last Week Tonight. But lately, he’s been popping up somewhere viewers never expected: daytime soap operas. After recent stints on General Hospital and Days of Our Lives, fans are calling for the comedian to make the jump from Primetime Emmys to Daytime Emmys....

item.title: X says top accounts steal videos from other users as it announces new video tools
item.description: Nikita Bier, X's head of product, said in a post on Monday that "[m]any videos from top accounts are simply stolen from other users, sometimes 5 years after they originally went viral," while noting that videos on the platform "make up close to half the impressions on X." According to Bier, X is launching a […]

item.title: Aoostar's mini PC completely destroys the Steam Machine on raw performance but you will have to add memory and storage
item.description: The Aoostar GODY 7600XT's impressive spec means you don't need to get lost in the lottery of Valve's queuing system.

item.title: Kai Cenat’s Streamer University is back, and the 2026 class is stacked
item.description: Kai Cenat revealed the Streamer University 2026 roster, including professors, students, club directors, and major creator names

item.title: Mysterious Compound Detected on Pluto and Titan
item.description: Something on Pluto and one of Saturn’s moons, Titan, absorbs light in a way unexplained by anything in spectroscopic databases.
